title: Global Internet access: trends and divides
visdescription: A scroller with three scenes, each  a line chart, showing the rise in Internet access between 2005 and 2025 for the world, regional groupings, and income groups
source: International Telecommunications Union (ITU). Retrieved from World Development Indicators (IT.NET.USER.ZS)
keyFactLabel: people worldwide do not use the Internet

scenes.text: [emphasis: Worldwide], the share of people who access the Internet has increased from 16 percent only two decades ago to over 70 percent today. Still, over a quarter of the global population (2.2 billion) remains offline.

Internet adoption mirrors an economy’s level of development: it is nearly universal (94 percent) in [emphasis: high-income countries] but less than a quarter (23 percent) in [emphasis: low-income countries].
Basic Internet access is lowest in [emphasis: Sub-Saharan Africa], where only around one-third of the population is online.

_meta.approvalNote: hand layouts; coverage_map is ranked-bars proxy not Mapbox
